query: NATO Drone Edge $40 billion counter-UAS initiative 2026 partners contracts

{"query":"NATO Drone Edge $40 billion counter-UAS initiative 2026 partners contracts","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1518,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.nato.int/en/news-and-events/articles/news/2026/07/07/nato-allies-invest-40-billion-dollars-in-counter-drone-capabilities-and-drone-training","title":"NATO Allies invest 40 billion dollars in counter-drone capabilities and drone training | NATO News","content":"On Tuesday (7 July 2026) at the NATO Summit Defence Industry Forum, NATO Allies announced that over 40 billion dollars will be invested in counter-drone capabilities over the next five years.
To support drone training, Allies will also leverage NATO’s multinational Flight Training Europe (NFTE) initiative.
At the Forum, Finland, France and Sweden joined the seventeen other NFTE members.
Through the initiative, participating Allies are granted access to the NFTE sixteen flight centres established across eight countries.
NATO’s Supply and Procurement Agency (NSPA) also awarded a contract in the hundreds of millions of dollars to procure surveillance drones for Allies."},{"url":"https://www.dronewatch.eu/nato-unveils-40-billion-investment-in-drones-and-counter-uas-capabilities/","title":"NATO unveils $40 billion investment in drones and counter-UAS capabilities - Dronewatch Europe","content":"NATO will invest more than $40 billion in drones and counter-unmanned aircraft systems (C-UAS) over the next five years.
The investment forms part of the newly launched NATO Drone Edge Initiative, which currently involves twenty Allied nations.
NATO also announced plans to create a dedicated counter-UAS marketplace, allowing member states to rapidly procure systems that have already been tested for NATO interoperability and compatibility.
Secretary General Rutte also introduced the NATO Front Door for Industry, a new platform intended to provide defence companies with a single point of access to NATO procurement opportunities, innovation programmes and partnership initiatives.
Although confirmed incidents remain relatively limited outside the direct vicinity of the war in Ukraine, the perception of an increasing drone threat has clearly accelerated investments in counter-UAS capabilities across Europe."},{"url":"https://www.nato.int/en/multimedia/multimedia/videos/2026/07/07/natos-drone-edge","title":"NATO’s Drone Edge | NATO Video","content":"Publication date 07 July 2026"},{"url":"https://united24media.com/world/nato-launches-40-billion-drone-edge-initiative-to-counter-drone-threats-20519","title":"NATO Launches $40 Billion Drone Edge Initiative to Counter Drone Threats — UNITED24 Media","content":"NATO launched the $40 billion Drone Edge initiative to strengthen counter-drone capabilities, drawing on lessons from the wars in Ukraine. ... NATO Launches $40 Billion Drone Edge Initiative to Counter Drone Threats. Jul 07, 2026 13:52 Updated Jul 07, 2026 14:27.Read more
NATO has launched a new $40 billion initiative aimed at strengthening the Alliance's ability to counter unmanned aerial threats, drawing on lessons learned from the wars in Ukraine and the Middle East.
Secretary General Mark Rutte announced the initiative, known as Drone Edge, during the NATO Defense Industry Forum held on July 7 alongside the Alliance's summit in Ankara, according to UNITED24 Media correspondents present at the event.
As part of the initiative, NATO will establish a dedicated counter-drone marketplace intended to help member states procure anti-drone systems more rapidly and at scale.
The Alliance also plans to expand drone pilot training through the NATO Flight Training Europe program.
According to Rutte, Finland, France, and Sweden have joined the project, bringing the total number of participating NATO members to 20."},{"url":"https://app.govly.com/public/signals/138866","title":"NATO Advances $40B Counter-Drone Initiative","date":"6 days ago","content":"6 days ago — NATO is progressing with its extensive $40 billion "Drone Edge" program aimed at deploying counter-drone systems across 20 member states ..."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"NATO Drone Edge $40 billion counter-UAS initiative 2026 partners contracts","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.armyrecognition.com/news/aerospace-news/2026/nato-approves-40-billion-counter-drone-initiative-to-defeat-low-cost-uav-threats","title":"NATO Approves $40 Billion Counter-Drone Initiative to Defeat Low-Cost UAV Threats","content":"* Man-Portable Air Defense Systems. - Analysis Defense and Security Industry. - Defence & Security Industry Technology 2026. - Air Force in the world. **NATO Allies will invest more than $40 billion over five years to expand counter-drone defenses, drone procurement, and operator training, NATO announced in Ankara on 7 July 2026, giving the Alliance a larger shield against one of the fastest-growing threats on modern battlefields. The plan adds a NATO counter-drone marketplace, expanded drone operator training under NATO Flight Training Europe, and a major procurement contract for surveillance drones through the NATO Support and Procurement Agency. By linking industry access, training capacity, and drone acquisition, the initiative aims to improve Allied readiness for high-intensity warfare where unmanned systems now shape reconnaissance, targeting, force protection, and air defense. NATO's new multi-year counter-drone initiative aims to expand Allied drone defence, operator training, and rapid procurement to counter the growing battlefield threat posed by low-cost unmanned aircraft (Picture source: U.S. DoW).","score":0.8810534,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://defence-industry.eu/nato-allies-to-invest-more-than-40-billion-in-counter-drone-systems-and-expand-drone-operator-training","title":"NATO Allies to invest more than $40 billion in counter-drone systems and expand drone operator training","content":"# NATO Allies to invest more than $40 billion in counter-drone systems and expand drone operator training. NATO Allies announced on 7 July 2026 that they will invest more than $40 billion in counter-drone capabilities over the next five years. The announcement was made at the NATO Summit Defence Industry Forum. To support faster procurement, NATO will establish a counter-drone marketplace. NATO said Allies will also use the multinational Flight Training Europe initiative to support drone operator training. NATO’s Support and Procurement Agency also awarded a contract worth hundreds of millions of dollars to procure surveillance drones for Allies. The company said the site will support rising demand from U.S. and allied customers as governments increase investment in counter-unmanned aircraft systems and short-range air defense.","score":0.8585411,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://united24media.com/world/nato-launches-40-billion-drone-edge-initiative-to-counter-drone-threats-20519","title":"NATO Launches $40 Billion Drone Edge Initiative to Counter Drone Threats — UNITED24 Media","content":"# NATO Launches $40 Billion Drone Edge Initiative to Counter Drone Threats. NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte attends a pre-summit press conference ahead of the 36th NATO Summit of Heads of State and Government at the Presidential Complex in Ankara, Türkiye on July 6, 2026. NATO has launched a new $40 billion initiative aimed at strengthening the Alliance's ability to counter unmanned aerial threats, drawing on lessons learned from the wars in Ukraine and the Middle East. Secretary General Mark Rutte announced the initiative, known as Drone Edge, during the NATO Defense Industry Forum held on July 7 alongside the Alliance's summit in Ankara, according to *UNITED24 Media* correspondents present at the event. Under the Drone Edge initiative, NATO member states will invest $40 billion over the next five years in counter-drone technologies and capabilities. Mark Rutte, Secretary General of NATO, speaks at the pre-summit press conference on July 6, 2026, in Ankara, Türkiye.","score":0.8358192,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.everythingrf.com/news/details/22323-nato-approves-40-billion-counter-drone-initiative-to-defeat-low-cost-uav-threats","title":"NATO Approves $40 Billion Counter-Drone Initiative to Defeat Low-Cost UAV Threats","content":"NATO has announced plans to invest more than $40 billion over the next five years to strengthen its counter-drone capabilities, expanding investments in drone procurement, operator training, and integrated defense systems as unmanned aerial threats continue to reshape modern warfare. Announced during a meeting in Ankara on 7 July 2026, the initiative represents one of the Alliance's largest coordinated efforts to improve its ability to detect, track, and defeat unmanned aircraft while increasing the use of drones across Allied forces. Alongside increased investment in counter-UAS technologies, the plan introduces a NATO counter-drone marketplace, expands drone operator training through NATO Flight Training Europe, and includes a major surveillance drone procurement program managed by the NATO Support and Procurement Agency (NSPA). The initiative is intended to strengthen NATO's readiness for future conflicts where unmanned systems are expected to play an increasingly important role in intelligence gathering, target acquisition, force protection, precision strikes, and air defense operations.
